title: From The Temporary To The Eternal Lessons From The Life Of The Apostle Paul
scriptureForWeek: A series of readings from Paul’s teachings and hope of the new heavens and the new earth as revealed in his Epistles and the book of Revelation.
weekTheme: Boys wish; men hope God will give us eternal joy What is heaven like? Is it all it’s cracked up to be? Is it really a place of comfort and joy, or just some wish or dream people made up to make them feel better about death? If one of your friends who doesn’t believe in Jesus asked you to share what you thought heaven is like, what would you say? If you were to ask one hundred people what they thought, you would probably get one hundred different answers. However, it's amazing that there would be one thing in common - people want to go there. Paul called heaven our true home. It’s the place we really belong to. He said it was our future hope and a place of glory. He even said that he would rather die and be with Christ because of how glorious it was, but he had stay on earth to help people know about the good news of the gospel. How is that for longing and hope? If you plan on going to heaven, you should probably know what you are getting into. You should get as much clarity as possible as to what it will be like, what you are going to be doing, how much interaction you will have with God, and who else is going to be there. The more you know, the more excited you will be. Just like anticipation for a vacation in an amazing place with people you love, you will begin to anticipate the joy and wonder of what your future actually holds.

days.title: On Earth As It Is In Heaven
days.scripture: 2 CORINTHIANS 5:1-10 For we know that when this earthly tent we live in is taken down (that is, when we die and leave this earthly body), we will have a house in heaven, an eternal body made for us by God himself and not by human hands. We grow weary in our present bodies, and we long to put on our heavenly bodies like new clothing. For we will put on heavenly bodies; we will not be spirits without bodies. While we live in these earthly bodies, we groan and sigh, but it’s not that we want to die and get rid of these bodies that clothe us. Rather, we want to put on our new bodies so that these dying bodies will be swallowed up by life. God himself has prepared us for this, and as a guarantee he has given us his Holy Spirit. So we are always confident, even though we know that as long as we live in these bodies we are not at home with the Lord. For we live by believing and not by seeing. Yes, we are fully confident, and we would rather be away from these earthly bodies, for then we will be at home with the Lord. So whether we are here in this body or away from this body, our goal is to please him. For we must all stand before Christ to be judged. We will each receive whatever we deserve for the good or evil we have done in this earthly body.
days.reflection: One of the big questions people often ask is, “Where is heaven?” Is it up in the sky? Is it some other realm? In what way does the spiritual realm interact with this one. What do you think? The answer may actually surprise you. It contains two parts to understand properly, so don’t zone out for the next few minutes. Focus in. First, what you think of as heaven and what our culture thinks of as heaven is not our final state of existence. Right now, people go to what is called “the intermediate state.” This is the paradise Jesus spoke about to the thief on the cross. It’s the spiritual realm where God and the angels are. This is not a physical place like we tend to think of, but it’s a real place, and it’s where God rules the universe from. This is still an amazing place of joy and peace, being directly in the presence of God and the people we love who knew God. Paul said it was “better by far” to be there. But this is not the final state of things. This is where it gets a little surprising. Second, heaven will actually be here on earth. It’s not going to be up in the sky somewhere, but it will actually be here on earth where God works to restore and renew all things. You may not have noticed this, but the Bible starts on earth, and the Bible ends on earth. Genesis 1:1-2 says, “In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. Now the earth was formless and empty, darkness was over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of God was hovering over the waters.” Revelation 21:1-4 says, “Then I saw “a new heaven and a new earth,” for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away, and there was no longer any sea. I saw the Holy City, the new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband. And I heard a loud voice from the throne saying, “Look! God’s dwelling place is now among the people, and he will dwell with them. They will be his people, and God himself will be with them and be their God.” That’s why Jesus taught us to pray that God's kingdom would come on earth as it is in heaven. He didn’t tell us to pray that God would evacuate us somewhere else, but that His world would invade this one. We are called to be people longing to bring heaven here on earth, not escape earth to go to heaven. We shouldn’t spend all our time trying to go somewhere else when God's agenda is to bring His life and love more fully here. As you move through your life, don’t fall into escapist thinking. Don’t get caught up living for the things of this earth. There is something better on the way. God is going to give you a new resurrected body that gets you ready to live in a new heaven and earth. Paul noted in today's reading that “we want to put on our new bodies so that these dying bodies will be swallowed up by life.” God is preparing you for something much better than just a good life now. He is getting you ready for an extraordinary life for eternity. Be patient and take heart; there is so much good in store for you.
questions: How is what the Bible teaches about heaven different from what our culture says about it? What about heaven from the Bible’s perspective is better than what the culture says?
Did anything in today’s lesson change your thinking about heaven? Why or why not?

Ask the person you are doing this study with how their view of heaven has changed over time. What did they used to think, and how has their view grown as they have learned more of what the Bible teaches?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. When you pray, don’t babble on and on as the Gentiles do. They think their prayers are answered merely by repeating their words again and again. Don’t be like them, for your Father knows exactly what you need even before you ask him! Pray like this: Our Father in heaven, may your name be kept holy. May your Kingdom come soon. May your will be done on earth, as it is in heaven. Matthew 6:7-10 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to bring His power to change anything happening in your life that isn’t going how you know He would want it to be. Take a moment to quiet your heart before God and surrender yourself to His will.

days.title: Citizens Of Heaven
days.scripture: PHILIPPIANS 3:18-21 For I have told you often before, and I say it again with tears in my eyes, that there are many whose conduct shows they are really enemies of the cross of Christ. They are headed for destruction. Their god is their appetite, they brag about shameful things, and they think only about this life here on earth. But we are citizens of heaven, where the Lord Jesus Christ lives. And we are eagerly waiting for him to return as our Savior. He will take our weak mortal bodies and change them into glorious bodies like his own, using the same power with which he will bring everything under his control.
days.reflection: One of the biggest issues in the world today is immigration and the plight of refugees. You may have seen some of this in the news. People are living in hellish conditions of war, poverty, and violence, and they want a better life. So they make long journeys to try and get into better countries. Sometimes it's dangerous boat rides across oceans, other times it’s a journey through the desert. When they arrive, they often get stuck at the border and turned away or sneak into the country, hoping not to get caught. The ultimate goal of immigrants is to try and gain new citizenship. It’s their desire to belong to a new country where they have all the rights and privileges of citizens and can build a new life of peace, safety, and prosperity. This is important because: Without citizenship, there is no real security. Without citizenship, the government is seen as a threat and not a help. Without citizenship, there is fear of being caught doing the wrong thing. Without citizenship, there is fear of deportation and being sent back to the old country. But gaining citizenship changes everything. Being a citizen gives you a sense of belonging. Being a citizen gives you protection from outside powers. Being a citizen lets you participate in every part of life without restriction. Being a citizen gives you help from the homeland. In the Roman Empire, gaining Roman citizenship was incredibly hard. People often had to bribe and pay large sums of money to be citizens. Paul was born a Roman citizen though. Citizenship gave him the right to vote, hold office, and the power of life and death over those in his family. When Paul died as a martyr, he was beheaded instead of crucified like Peter because citizens were exempt from crucifixion. Being a citizen had real perks, even at death. Paul goes on to say that his real citizenship wasn’t in Rome. It wasn’t on earth. It was in heaven. That is amazing. When you are a citizen of heaven, you have a right to be there. You have a sense of belonging. You are protected and safe. You can build your life and prosper. This is the most incredible news a man can ever know in his heart. That he is a citizen of heaven. He belongs. Even the best citizenship on earth is just a preview of our true home. When you get to heaven, you won’t have to sneak in. You won’t have to beg and plead and make your case before God. You can walk in welcomed and wanted because this is your true home. A man with that confidence in his future will be a helpful and hopeful man here. Where is your citizenship young man?
questions: If you could be a citizen in any country of the world, where would it be and why?
How does being a citizen of your country shape how you view the rest of the world? How does being a citizen of heaven change how we see the world?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share what being a citizen of heaven means to them. How does this help them deal with the political drama in our world today?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Above all, you must live as citizens of heaven, conducting yourselves in a manner worthy of the Good News about Christ. Then, whether I come and see you again or only hear about you, I will know that you are standing together with one spirit and one purpose, fighting together for the faith, which is the Good News.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for your citizenship in heaven. Ask Him to make the implications of that fact more real to you today.

days.title: Face To Face
days.scripture: 1 CORINTHIANS 13:11-13 When I was a child, I spoke and thought and reasoned as a child. But when I grew up, I put away childish things. Now we see things imperfectly, like puzzling reflections in a mirror, but then we will see everything with perfect clarity. All that I know now is partial and incomplete, but then I will know everything completely, just as God now knows me completely. Three things will last forever—faith, hope, and love—and the greatest of these is love.
days.reflection: 1 Corinthians 13 is one of the most famous chapters in the whole Bible. You probably know it by heart. Love is patient, love is kind...that one. But at the end of the chapter, there is a really interesting verse that gives us a clue about the wonder of what heaven will be like. Did you notice it in today’s reading? Look back over it and see if you can spot it. Here it is: Now we see things imperfectly, like puzzling reflections in a mirror, but then we will see everything with perfect clarity. That’s it. Paul says that our current view of God and spiritual realities are puzzling. It’s like looking in a dirty mirror where there is a rough outline of what’s happening, but you can't see anything with clarity or detail. BUT he says a day is coming when we will see everything in perfect clarity. This is one of the most amazing aspects of heaven. We will be able to see God clearly, creation clearly, and ourselves clearly. No more sin, no more distortion, no more lies. Look at how John the disciple put it in the book of Revelation: No longer will there be any curse. The throne of God and of the Lamb will be in the city, and his servants will serve him. They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads. There will be no more night. They will not need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, for the Lord God will give them light. And they will reign for ever and ever. Revelation 22:3-5 It says, “we will see His face.” I want you to try and comprehend that. I want you to imagine being able to look into the face of God himself and that you are going to get a kind of face tattoo. It says that His name is going to be written on your head. That is very cool. It also says something else that’s just wild. It says that you will reign forever and ever. That’s right. God doesn’t bring you to heaven so you can grovel before Him in sadness. He brings you there so you can rule and reign in gladness. Much like the story of the kids in Narnia. On Earth, they are just ordinary kids, but in Narnia they are kings and queens. The same is true of the age to come. You are destined to see God clearly and serve Him fearlessly. Most people in our world today have very distorted pictures of God. They either make Him like an old Santa Clause who trots around giving gifts and being nice, or they paint Him as a divine cop who is setting up speed traps to catch us when we break His laws. They have a distorted view of heaven. White robes, signing, boring. But imagine seeing God clearly! Face to face! Eye to eye! Knowing His heart, His smile, His power, His love. Today you have seen that heaven is about clarity. It’s about seeing who God really is and seeing your true purpose. Keep looking intently, young man. Don’t let the world block or blur the vision you have. Don’t get distracted or discouraged. You will see His face, and you will reign in heaven. Life is preparation for knowing God and growing in leadership. Practice is in session. Train well.
questions: What do you think God's face might look like? What sort of mood do you think He will be in when you meet Him?
What changes do you think will have to happen for us to be able to see God clearly? Why can’t we do that now?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share what they are most looking forward to about meeting with God face to face. Are there any things they are nervous about?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. All praise to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. It is by his great mercy that we have been born again, because God raised Jesus Christ from the dead. Now we live with great expectation, and we have a priceless inheritance—an inheritance that is kept in heaven for you, pure and undefiled, beyond the reach of change and decay. And through your faith, God is protecting you by his power until you receive this salvation, which is ready to be revealed on the last day for all to see. 1 Peter 1:3-5 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for the amazing future you have ahead of you. Ask Him to fill your heart with hope and expectation for that.

days.title: Better Than You Imagined
days.scripture: 1 CORINTHIANS 2:9-10 That is what the Scriptures mean when they say, “No eye has seen, no ear has heard, and no mind has imagined what God has prepared for those who love him.” But it was to us that God revealed these things by his Spirit. For his Spirit searches out everything and shows us God’s deep secrets.
days.reflection: What’s the coolest place on earth you can imagine visiting? Maybe it's Hawaii. Beaches, surf, seafood, girls. Maybe it's Alaska. Wild forests, hunting, grizzlies, fishing. Maybe Iceland? Rugged coasts, volcanos, Vikings. Maybe it’s a cool city, like New York or LA. Sports, pizza, culture, energy. Where would you go if you could go anywhere? Now, how much do you look forward to living in the new heavens and the new earth? I think for many guys your age, one of the greatest fears is that heaven won’t be as good as we hope. That it will somehow be a bit of a letdown. I think a lot of guys your age have a sense that it’s probably more fun here and now. I mean, what could be better than what this life offers? Having sex, traveling with friends, and having some awesome adventures. Getting to make money and buy the stuff you always wanted. Could heaven really be better than that? Paul would answer emphatically "YES! In today’s reading, he says that what is ahead is better than we can even dream. It's more than our eyes, ears, and minds can imagine. YET, the Holy Spirit gives us a preview of what it's like. Have you been to a movie in the theaters, arrived early, and watched the trailers for new movies coming out? A good trailer will make you want to see the whole thing. That’s what the Bible is doing in giving us a preview of what’s to come. It’s just a taste to create a hunger for more. Here is a bit about what is ahead in the new heavens and new earth. (For a deeper view, check out Revelation chapters 20, 21, and 22) God is there. You will see Him face to face. He will share His joy, power, and love with you. His presence will be so strong that they won’t even need lights there. There is no more curse. You won’t be stuck with the sin, futility, and stupidity that make life so hard now. There will be no more tears, crying, heartache, death, or grief. Those things will be wiped away and banished forever. You will have a new body. You will be given a new resurrected body like Jesus. It will not age or face death. Jesus could do some cool stuff when He rose from the dead. You will be able to as well. The food will be incredible. Heaven is described as a massive feast. Sweet drinks, fatty meats, and the best of all desserts. The whole thing will be kicked off as a huge celebration. Heaven is described as a wedding celebration. The Church (us) and the Groom (Jesus) will be together, celebrating our eternal relationship. Dancing, laughing, feasting, drinking. That will just kick things off for eternity. You will live in an amazing city. Heaven is described as a city that’s almost 1500 miles squared. That’s a city that is almost as big as the land mass of the United States. The city has a river running through it, and it will be filled with the most fun and interesting projects and people. There will be a new earth. Heaven is the capital city of the new earth, but the earth will still have nations and renewed creation to explore and cultivate. The people we love the most will be there. Heaven will be a joyful reunion of our dearest friends and family who had given their lives to Jesus and received His grace. The animals will be more like pets than predators. The lion and lamb will lie down together. There will be harmony in the created order. Animals will be playing tag, not trying to eat each other. That all sounds unreal. Jesus said that He was going to prepare a place for us, and that when it was ready, He would come back to take us to be with Him. Your job is to get yourself ready to become the kind of man who can live in that kind of world. A world that is really going to be better than the stuff of this earth, and more than you can imagine. You do not want to miss out on that.
questions: Of all the things you have heard the Bible teach about the new heavens and the new earth, what is most interesting to you?
What are you most looking forward to?
What do you still have questions about?
Ask the person you are doing this study with what questions they are going to ask God when they meet Him.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. But God is so rich in mercy, and he loved us so much, that even though we were dead because of our sins, he gave us life when he raised Christ from the dead. (It is only by God’s grace that you have been saved!) For he raised us from the dead along with Christ and seated us with him in the heavenly realms because we are united with Christ Jesus. So God can point to us in all future ages as examples of the incredible wealth of his grace and kindness toward us, as shown in all he has done for us who are united with Christ Jesus. God saved you by his grace when you believed. And you can’t take credit for this; it is a gift from God. Salvation is not a reward for the good things we have done, so none of us can boast about it. Ephesians 2:4-9 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to fill your heart with hope and anticipation for heaven. Ask the Holy Spirit to keep revealing to you all that is in store.

days.title: How To Get To Heaven
days.scripture: 1 CORINTHIANS 15:17-28 And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile; you are still in your sins. Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ are lost. If only for this life we have hope in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied. But Christ has indeed been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep. For since death came through a man, the resurrection of the dead comes also through a man. For as in Adam all die, so in Christ all will be made alive. But each in turn: Christ, the firstfruits; then, when he comes, those who belong to him. Then the end will come, when he hands over the kingdom to God the Father after he has destroyed all dominion, authority and power. For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet. The last enemy to be destroyed is death. For he “has put everything under his feet.” Now when it says that “everything” has been put under him, it is clear that this does not include God himself, who put everything under Christ. When he has done this, then the Son himself will be made subject to him who put everything under him, so that God may be all in all.
days.reflection: This week you have spent time looking at the reality of the new heavens and the new earth. Paul taught that what lies ahead is so incredible that we can hardly comprehend it. It's based on what he called the gospel. The gospel is the good news of how God sent his son Jesus into the world to take away sin and death, which is a result of Satan’s temptation and Adam and Eve's rebellion. Jesus lived the life we should have lived, and died the death we should have died, to free us from our sin. Our sin was put upon Jesus on the cross as our substitute, and we received His righteousness as a divine exchange (just a note: the best heroes in movies are the ones who sacrifice themselves for others out of love. These heroes are really based on what Jesus did for us). The Bible teaches that the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life. Because Jesus paid for our sin, death was no longer able to hold Him down. Jesus rose from the dead, something we celebrate at Easter, and now gives us His life as a gift. He goes to prepare a place for us so we can live with Him forever in the kind of world we have always dreamed about. AND YET, this isn’t just some religious story. It’s not a “take it or leave it” story. It’s not a “you do you” story. Paul says that the resurrection of Jesus is a historical fact. It’s either the most important thing that has ever happened, or it’s a total lie. There is nothing in between. Paul wrote this: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile; you are still in your sins...If only for this life we have hope in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied. Without Jesus' resurrection, our faith is a waste of time; nothing can really remove our guilt and shame. If this is all just a story that’s been made up to make us feel better about things here, Paul says that we should be pitied above everyone else because we have been deceived. Whoa! These are huge claims. You see, if Jesus is who He says He is, and He really died for our sins and rose again, then this is the most important thing in all the world. And it’s true. That’s why history is divided in two; before Jesus’ life and after it. Here are just a few of the many reasons to believe He did rise from the dead: The empty tomb The appearances of Jesus to His disciples The transformation of the disciples from scared followers to fearless disciples Jesus' impact on history The experience of billions of people who have been changed by Him The life, death, and resurrection of Jesus are what make the new heavens and the new earth possible. The people who get to go to heaven and be a part of God's creation get there only one way; trusting in Jesus and what He has done. Being good won’t get you into heaven. Trying harder won’t get you into heaven. Even doing religious things won’t get you there. There is only one way. Trust in what Jesus has done for you. It's believing in His work on your behalf. It's looking to Him and not your own good works. It's turning away from your sin and being your own boss and giving yourself to Him. Jesus is the way, the truth, and the life. Trusting in Him is what gets you there. So, what are you waiting for? Is anything holding you back? Repent of your sin, believe the gospel, and get ready for grace to change your life, both now and for eternity.
questions: If you could talk to anyone from the Bible when you get to heaven, who would it be and why? What questions would you want to ask them?
Do you think Jesus rose from the dead? Why or why not? Do you agree with what Paul said about it being an all-or-nothing proposition?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to list a few reasons why they believe Jesus rose from the dead. What difference has the truth of the resurrection made to their faith?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life through Christ Jesus our Lord. Romans 6:23 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for the gospel and all that Jesus has done. If you haven’t put your trust in Him, now is a great time.

extras: WEEK 8 MANSCHOOL THE FEAST MAN SCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. THE FEAST One of the most important things you can do for your son is give him a compelling vision of heaven. The right vision of the new heavens and earth is a potent force in a man’s life. It can help him resist temptation, become a better steward of his resources, deal with difficulty and suffering based on what is ahead, and help him grow in his relationship with Jesus. So, this week you are going to show him heaven is a feast. John Ortberg wrote, “We must arrange our lives so that sin no longer looks good to us” Go all out here. Beware the soul-sucking voice of reasonableness. Cook or order your son’s... Favorite foods. Favorite deserts. Set the room up so it looks special and cool. Play his favorite music while you eat. Then read this verse to him and explain what the kingdom of God is actually like. In Jerusalem, the LORD of Heaven’s Armies will spread a wonderful feast for all the people of the world. It will be a delicious banquet with clear, well- aged wine and choice meat. There he will remove the cloud of gloom, the shadow of death that hangs over the earth. He will swallow up death forever! The Sovereign LORD will wipe away all tears. He will remove forever all insults and mockery against his land and people. The LORD has spoken! In that day the people will proclaim, “This is our God! We trusted in him, and he saved us! This is the LORD, in whom we trusted. Let us rejoice in the salvation he brings!” Isaiah 25:6-9
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
